---
title: "36-460/660 Final Project - Catcher Blocking Analysis"
author: "Arthur Spirou"
date: 04/28/2026
format: pdf
---

Gen AI Usage Statement: This notebook was produced with the use of Claude Code for VS Code

# ── Load & Process the Data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)

sc_2025_raw <- readRDS("statcast_2025_raw.rds")
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#build_savant_url + windows + pull_savant_chunk functions

library(httr)
library(tidyverse)

# Pull data from Baseball Savant (skip if loading from RDS above)
build_savant_url <- function(start, end) {
  paste0(
    "https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/statcast_search/csv?all=true",
    "&player_type=pitcher",
    "&game_date_gt=", start,
    "&game_date_lt=", end,
    "&type=details",
    "&hfSea=2025%7C",
    "&game_type=R"
  )
}

# 3-day windows
breaks <- seq(as.Date("2025-03-27"), as.Date("2025-09-28"), by = "3 days")

windows <- tibble(
  start = breaks,
  end   = c(breaks[-1] - 1, as.Date("2025-09-28"))
)

# Check how many chunks we'll be making
nrow(windows)  # should be ~62 chunks

pull_savant_chunk <- function(start, end, retries = 3) {
  url <- build_savant_url(start, end)
  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")

  for (i in seq_len(retries)) {
    resp <- tryCatch(
      GET(url, write_disk(tmp, overwrite = TRUE),
          timeout(300),
          add_headers(
            `User-Agent`      = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36",
            `Accept`          = "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
            `Accept-Language` = "en-US,en;q=0.5"
          )),
      error = function(e) { message("Attempt ", i, " failed: ", e$message); NULL }
    )

    if (!is.null(resp) && status_code(resp) == 200) {
      df <- read_csv(tmp, show_col_types = FALSE)
      if ("pitch_type" %in% names(df)) {
        message("Got ", nrow(df), " pitches: ", start, " – ", end)
        return(df)
      }
    }
    message("Retrying in 10s...")
    Sys.sleep(10)
  }
  warning("All retries failed for ", start, " to ", end)
  return(NULL)
}
```
```{r}
#| eval: false
#test pull

test <- pull_savant_chunk("2025-03-27", "2025-03-29")
nrow(test)  # should be well under 25,000
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#full season pull

sc_2025_raw <- pmap_dfr(windows, function(start, end) {
  pull_savant_chunk(as.character(start), as.character(end))
}) %>%
  mutate(game_date = as.Date(game_date, origin = "1970-01-01"))
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
#date fix + sanity check + saveRDS

# Fix date column, verify, and save
sc_2025_raw <- sc_2025_raw %>%
  mutate(game_date = as.Date(game_date, origin = "1970-01-01"))

cat("Total rows:", nrow(sc_2025_raw), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(sc_2025_raw), "\n")
cat("Date range:", as.character(min(sc_2025_raw$game_date)),
    "to", as.character(max(sc_2025_raw$game_date)), "\n")
table(sc_2025_raw$description) |> sort(decreasing = TRUE) |> head(10)

saveRDS(sc_2025_raw, "statcast_2025_raw.rds")
```

# ── PITCH BLOCKING EDA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

```{r}
# explore variables + pitch blocking labels

# All column names
cat("=== All variables ===\n")
print(names(sc_2025_raw))

# Check description values — wild_pitch and passed_ball should appear here
cat("\n=== All values of 'description' ===\n")
sort(table(sc_2025_raw$description), decreasing = TRUE) |> print()

# Check 'events' column — records the event that ended a play (e.g., wild_pitch, passed_ball)
cat("\n=== All values of 'events' (non-NA) ===\n")
sc_2025_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(events)) %>%
  count(events, sort = TRUE) %>%
  print(n = 50)

# Scan all column names for blocking-relevant keywords
cat("\n=== Columns with 'wild', 'pass', 'block', or 'bounce' in name ===\n")
names(sc_2025_raw)[grepl("wild|pass|block|bounce", names(sc_2025_raw), ignore.case = TRUE)] |> print()

# Quick count of wild pitches and passed balls specifically
cat("\n=== Wild pitches and passed balls in 'events' ===\n")
sc_2025_raw %>%
  filter(events %in% c("wild_pitch", "passed_ball")) %>%
  count(events)
```

```{r}
sum(grepl("wild pitch", sc_2025_raw$des, ignore.case = TRUE), na.rm = TRUE)
sum(grepl("passed ball", sc_2025_raw$des, ignore.case = TRUE), na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# blocking opportunity set + outcome coding
#
# Opportunity filter: plate_z < sz_bot - block_depth_threshold
#   Requires the pitch to be at least block_depth_threshold feet below the
#   batter-specific zone bottom. The stricter cutoff reduces the number of
#   marginal pitches that catchers can receive cleanly with the glove,
#   making is_block == 0 more likely to reflect a true non-block rather
#   than a routine glove catch. See EDA Fig 4 for the empirical motivation.
#
# block_depth_threshold = 0.3 ft (~3.6 inches). Chosen based on Fig 4,
#   which shows block rate rising steeply beyond this depth. Pitches at
#   0–0.3 ft below the zone have block rates near 0–2% and are likely
#   received cleanly; the curve begins accelerating around 0.3 ft.
#   Analogous to shadow_threshold in the framing model.
#
# Outcome: is_block == 1 if the pitch was recorded as a successful block
#          (blocked_ball or swinging_strike_blocked); 0 otherwise.
#   NOTE: the 0 class still includes some clean receives; this limitation
#   is acknowledged but substantially reduced relative to plate_z < sz_bot.

block_depth_threshold <- 0.3  # ft below zone bottom (~3.6 inches)

blocking_df <- sc_2025_raw |>
  filter(
    !is.na(plate_z), !is.na(sz_bot), !is.na(sz_top),
    !is.na(plate_x), !is.na(fielder_2),
    !is.na(pitch_type), !is.na(release_speed)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    depth      = sz_bot - plate_z,
    below_zone = plate_z < sz_bot - block_depth_threshold,
    is_block   = as.integer(description %in%
                   c("blocked_ball", "swinging_strike_blocked")),
    swing      = as.integer(description == "swinging_strike_blocked"),
    catcher_id = as.factor(fielder_2),
    pitcher_id = as.factor(pitcher),
    pitch_type = as.factor(pitch_type),
    count      = as.factor(paste0(balls, "-", strikes)),
    p_throws   = as.factor(p_throws)
  ) |>
  filter(below_zone)
```

```{r}
# blocking opportunity sanity checks

cat("Blocking opportunities (plate_z < sz_bot):", nrow(blocking_df), "\n")
cat("Successful blocks   (is_block == 1):", sum(blocking_df$is_block), "\n")
cat("Non-blocks          (is_block == 0):", sum(blocking_df$is_block == 0), "\n")
cat("Block rate:", round(mean(blocking_df$is_block), 3), "\n")
cat("Unique catchers:", n_distinct(blocking_df$catcher_id), "\n")
cat("Unique pitchers:", n_distinct(blocking_df$pitcher_id), "\n")

# pitch-type breakdown among opportunities
blocking_df |> count(pitch_type, sort = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# EDA Fig 1: block rate heatmap by pitch location
# Uses absolute plate_x / plate_z; dashed line marks avg zone bottom.
# Shows block rate rising as pitches get lower and more horizontally extreme.

avg_sz_bot <- mean(blocking_df$sz_bot)

block_heatmap_plot <- blocking_df %>%
  mutate(
    px_bin = round(plate_x / 0.1) * 0.1,
    pz_bin = round(plate_z / 0.1) * 0.1
  ) %>%
  group_by(px_bin, pz_bin) %>%
  summarise(block_rate = mean(is_block), n_pitches = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_pitches >= 20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = px_bin, y = pz_bin, fill = block_rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = avg_sz_bot,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = -2.1, y = avg_sz_bot + 0.06,
           label = "avg. zone\nbottom", hjust = 0, color = "black", size = 2.8) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "dodgerblue",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "firebrick",
    midpoint = mean(blocking_df$is_block),
    name     = "Block rate"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-2.5, 2.5)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Recorded block rate by pitch location (2025 below-zone pitches)",
    subtitle = "Dashed line = average strike zone bottom; lower pitches show higher recorded block rates",
    x        = "Horizontal position (ft, catcher's perspective)",
    y        = "Vertical position (ft)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

block_heatmap_plot
```

```{r}
# EDA Fig 2: below-zone opportunities per catcher
# Motivates the 100-pitch minimum cutoff.

n_total_catchers <- n_distinct(blocking_df$catcher_id)
n_eligible       <- blocking_df |> count(catcher_id) |> filter(n >= 100) |> nrow()

block_pitchdist_plot <- blocking_df %>%
  count(catcher_id, name = "n_pitches") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_pitches)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 100, color = "firebrick",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 115, y = Inf, label = "100 pitch\nminimum",
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5, color = "firebrick", size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title    = "Below-zone pitches per catcher (2025 season)",
    subtitle = paste0(n_total_catchers, " total catchers; ",
                      n_eligible, " meet the 100-pitch minimum"),
    x        = "Number of below-zone pitches",
    y        = "Number of catchers"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

block_pitchdist_plot
```

```{r}
# EDA Fig 3: block rate by pitch type
# Shows breaking balls have higher recorded block rates than fastballs.
# Motivates including pitch_type as a fixed effect and collapsing sparse types.

block_by_pitchtype_plot <- blocking_df %>%
  group_by(pitch_type) %>%
  summarise(
    block_rate = mean(is_block),
    n_pitches  = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_pitches >= 100) %>%
  mutate(pitch_type = fct_reorder(pitch_type, block_rate)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = block_rate, y = pitch_type, fill = block_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(scales::percent(block_rate, accuracy = 0.1),
                       "  (n=", scales::comma(n_pitches), ")")),
    hjust = -0.05, size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "dodgerblue",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "firebrick",
    midpoint = mean(blocking_df$is_block),
    guide    = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.3))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Recorded block rate by pitch type (2025 below-zone pitches)",
    subtitle = "Breaking balls show higher block rates; sparse pitch types will be collapsed before modeling",
    x        = "Recorded block rate",
    y        = "Pitch type"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

block_by_pitchtype_plot
```

```{r}
# EDA Fig 4: block rate by depth below the zone
# Uses ALL below-zone pitches (no depth threshold) so the full 0–1.5 ft range
# is visible and the 0.1 ft cutoff annotation sits inside the data.
# Key figure: motivates the tighter opportunity filter.

block_by_depth_plot <- sc_2025_raw %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(plate_z), !is.na(sz_bot), !is.na(fielder_2),
    plate_z < sz_bot
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    depth     = sz_bot - plate_z,
    depth_bin = round(depth / 0.05) * 0.05,
    is_block  = as.integer(description %in%
                  c("blocked_ball", "swinging_strike_blocked"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(depth_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    block_rate = mean(is_block),
    n_pitches  = n(),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_pitches >= 50, depth_bin >= 0, depth_bin <= 1.5) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = depth_bin, y = block_rate)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n_pitches), color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.3, color = "firebrick",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.32, y = Inf,
           label = "0.3 ft cutoff",
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1.3, color = "firebrick", size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Pitches", range = c(1, 5),
                        labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Recorded block rate by depth below zone (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "Block rate rises sharply as pitches move further below the zone bottom",
    x        = "Depth below batter-specific zone bottom (ft)",
    y        = "Recorded block rate"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

block_by_depth_plot
```

```{r}
# eligible catchers + blocking model dataset
# Using 100-pitch minimum (blocking opps are rarer than framing opps)
#
# Iterative filter: the joint catcher+pitcher filter can shrink some groups
# below their threshold. Repeat until the dataset stabilises.

blocking_model_df <- blocking_df |>
  filter(depth <= 1.5)   # cap: pitches > 1.5 ft below zone are near-certain blocks
                         # and cause GLM to predict p ≈ 1, which crashes glmer PIRLS

repeat {
  old_n <- nrow(blocking_model_df)

  eligible_blockers <- blocking_model_df |> count(catcher_id) |> filter(n >= 100) |> pull(catcher_id) |> as.character()
  eligible_pitchers <- blocking_model_df |> count(pitcher_id) |> filter(n >= 20)  |> pull(pitcher_id) |> as.character()

  blocking_model_df <- blocking_model_df |>
    filter(as.character(catcher_id) %in% eligible_blockers,
           as.character(pitcher_id) %in% eligible_pitchers)

  if (nrow(blocking_model_df) == old_n) break
}

blocking_model_df <- blocking_model_df |>
  mutate(catcher_id = droplevels(catcher_id),
         pitcher_id = droplevels(pitcher_id))

cat("Pitches in blocking model dataset:", nrow(blocking_model_df), "\n")
cat("Catchers in blocking model:", n_distinct(blocking_model_df$catcher_id), "\n")
cat("Pitchers in blocking model:", n_distinct(blocking_model_df$pitcher_id), "\n")
```

```{r}
# collapse rare pitch types into "Other" before modeling
# Pitch types with fewer than 500 below-zone pitches are too sparse to
# estimate reliably; collapsing avoids separation and unstable coefficients.
# Note: swing removed as a predictor — swing==1 implies is_block==1 by
# construction (swinging_strike_blocked is a success label), causing
# quasi-complete separation and optimizer divergence.

common_types <- blocking_model_df |>
  count(pitch_type) |>
  filter(n >= 500) |>
  pull(pitch_type) |>
  as.character()

blocking_model_df <- blocking_model_df |>
  mutate(
    pitch_type_collapsed = fct_other(pitch_type, keep = common_types,
                                     other_level = "Other")
  )

cat("Pitch type levels after collapsing:\n")
print(levels(blocking_model_df$pitch_type_collapsed))
```

# ── PITCH BLOCKING MODELING────────────────────────────────────────────────────

```{r}
# simple blocking model
# Fixed effects: pitch location (plate_x, plate_z), pitch type (collapsed)
# Random effects: catcher, pitcher
# Note: swing dropped — quasi-complete separation (swing=1 => is_block=1 always)

library(lme4)

block_mod_simple <- glmer(
  is_block ~ scale(plate_x) + scale(plate_z) + pitch_type_collapsed +
             (1 | catcher_id) + (1 | pitcher_id),
  data    = blocking_model_df,
  family  = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                         optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

summary(block_mod_simple)
```

```{r}
# full blocking model
# Adds: release_speed, count, p_throws (kept as candidate; model comparison decides)

block_mod_full <- glmer(
  is_block ~ scale(plate_x) + scale(plate_z) + pitch_type_collapsed +
             scale(release_speed) + count + p_throws +
             (1 | catcher_id) + (1 | pitcher_id),
  data    = blocking_model_df,
  family  = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                         optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

summary(block_mod_full)
```

```{r}
# model comparison: AIC + likelihood ratio test

aic_comparison <- AIC(block_mod_simple, block_mod_full)
print(aic_comparison)

lrt_result <- anova(block_mod_simple, block_mod_full, test = "Chisq")
print(lrt_result)
```

```{r}
# model comparison summary table (report-ready)
# Run after the anova chunk above so aic_comparison and lrt_result exist.

block_model_comparison_table <- tibble(
  Model            = c("Simple model", "Full model"),
  `Fixed effects`  = c(
    "plate_x + plate_z + pitch_type",
    "+ release_speed + count + p_throws"
  ),
  `Random effects` = c(
    "Catcher + Pitcher",
    "Catcher + Pitcher"
  ),
  AIC              = round(aic_comparison$AIC, 1),
  `Delta AIC`      = round(aic_comparison$AIC - min(aic_comparison$AIC), 1),
  `p-value`        = c("—", format.pval(lrt_result$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2], digits = 3))
) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    format  = "simple",
    caption = "Blocking model comparison: simple vs. full mixed-effects logistic regression",
    digits  = 1
  )

block_model_comparison_table

# Select the winning model for downstream use
# Update this assignment after seeing AIC results
block_mod_final <- if (aic_comparison["block_mod_full", "AIC"] <
                       aic_comparison["block_mod_simple", "AIC"]) {
  block_mod_full
} else {
  block_mod_simple
}

cat("Selected model:", ifelse(identical(block_mod_final, block_mod_full),
                              "full", "simple"), "\n")
```

```{r}
# catcher ranking: blocks above average per 100 opportunities
#
# Conversion from log-odds random effect to interpretable metric:
#   1. Get model intercept (log-odds baseline for reference category)
#   2. For each catcher: P(block | catcher) = plogis(intercept + u_catcher)
#   3. League baseline: P(block | average catcher) = plogis(intercept)
#   4. Blocks above average per 100 opp = (P_catcher - P_baseline) * 100
#
# Scaling by 100 gives "blocks above average per 100 blocking opportunities."
# Savant uses a different outcome (WP/PB prevention) and broader opportunity
# definition, so direct scale comparison is not meaningful; rank correlation is.

intercept <- fixef(block_mod_final)[["(Intercept)"]]
p_baseline <- plogis(intercept)

catcher_effects <- ranef(block_mod_final)$catcher_id %>%
  rownames_to_column("catcher_id") %>%
  rename(random_effect = `(Intercept)`) %>%
  mutate(
    p_catcher   = plogis(intercept + random_effect),
    baa_per_100 = (p_catcher - p_baseline) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(baa_per_100))

cat("League baseline block rate (model intercept):",
    round(p_baseline, 3), "\n")
cat("Range of catcher effects (BAA/100 opp):",
    round(min(catcher_effects$baa_per_100), 3), "to",
    round(max(catcher_effects$baa_per_100), 3), "\n")

head(catcher_effects, 20)
```


```{r}
# catcher name lookup
# fielder_2 in Statcast = MLB MLBAM player ID. Names are not in the raw data.
# Uses baseballr::mlb_people() if available; falls back to numeric IDs.

catcher_effects_named <- tryCatch({
  library(baseballr)
  ids <- as.integer(catcher_effects$catcher_id)
  lookup <- map_dfr(ids, function(id) {
    tryCatch(mlb_people(id)[ , c("id", "full_name")],
             error = function(e) tibble(id = id, full_name = NA_character_))
  })
  catcher_effects %>%
    mutate(player_id = as.integer(catcher_id)) %>%
    left_join(lookup, by = c("player_id" = "id")) %>%
    mutate(label = if_else(is.na(full_name), as.character(catcher_id), full_name))
}, error = function(e) {
  message("baseballr unavailable — using numeric IDs: ", e$message)
  catcher_effects %>% mutate(label = as.character(catcher_id))
})

head(catcher_effects_named)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
# bootstrap timing test — 422 sec/iteration (7 min) rules out sequential bootstrapping
# (B=200 ≈ 23 hrs, B=100 ≈ 12 hrs). Using arm::se.ranef() instead.

boot_fun <- function(model) {
  intercept  <- fixef(model)[["(Intercept)"]]
  p_baseline <- plogis(intercept)
  re         <- ranef(model)$catcher_id
  u          <- re[["(Intercept)"]]
  names(u)   <- rownames(re)
  (plogis(intercept + u) - p_baseline) * 100
}

system.time({
  bootMer(block_mod_full, FUN = boot_fun, nsim = 1,
          type = "parametric", use.u = FALSE)
})
```

```{r}
# uncertainty quantification: conditional SEs from arm::se.ranef()
#
# bootMer() timed at 422 sec/iteration — infeasible for B>=100.
# Instead: arm::se.ranef() extracts the conditional SE for each catcher BLUP
# from the curvature of the Laplace-approximated posterior. This is the
# standard mixed-models approach when bootstrap is computationally prohibitive.
# CIs = BLUP ± 1.645 * SE (90%). Treats variance parameters as known; slight
# undercoverage for sparse catchers but appropriate for this sample size range.

library(arm)

catcher_se <- se.ranef(block_mod_full)$catcher_id |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("catcher_id") |>
  rename(se_u = `(Intercept)`)

intercept <- fixef(block_mod_full)[["(Intercept)"]]
p_baseline <- plogis(intercept)

n_opps_df <- blocking_model_df |>
  count(catcher_id, name = "n_opps") |>
  mutate(catcher_id = as.character(catcher_id))

catcher_effects_named <- catcher_effects_named |>
  left_join(catcher_se, by = "catcher_id") |>
  left_join(n_opps_df, by = "catcher_id") |>
  mutate(
    ci_lo = (plogis(intercept + random_effect - 1.645 * se_u) - p_baseline) * 100,
    ci_hi = (plogis(intercept + random_effect + 1.645 * se_u) - p_baseline) * 100
  )

cat("SE range:", round(range(catcher_effects_named$se_u), 4), "\n")
cat("Median CI width (BAA/100 opp):", round(median(catcher_effects_named$ci_hi - catcher_effects_named$ci_lo), 3), "\n")
```

```{r}
# caterpillar plot with 90% CI error bars
# Primary results figure. CIs from arm::se.ranef() conditional SEs.
# Catchers whose CI excludes 0 are reliably above/below average.

block_caterpillar_ci_plot <- catcher_effects_named |>
  mutate(
    label     = fct_reorder(label, baa_per_100),
    direction = if_else(baa_per_100 >= 0, "Above average", "Below average")
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = baa_per_100, y = label, color = direction)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                 height = 0.4, linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Above average" = "steelblue", "Below average" = "firebrick"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Catcher blocking: blocks above average per 100 opportunities (2025 season)",
    subtitle = paste0("Based on ", nrow(catcher_effects_named),
                      " catchers with ≥ 100 below-zone pitches (0.3–1.5 ft below zone);\n",
                      "error bars = 90% CI from conditional SEs (Laplace approximation)"),
    x        = "Blocks above average per 100 opportunities",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 6),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )

block_caterpillar_ci_plot
```

```{r}
# ridge plot: conditional normal distributions N(u_j, SE_j^2) per catcher
#
# Pre-compute density over a grid for each catcher, then pass to geom_ridgeline.
# geom_ridgeline with stat="function" doesn't support per-row aes() args.

library(ggridges)

x_grid <- seq(-6, 7, by = 0.02)

ridge_data <- catcher_effects_named |>
  mutate(
    label  = fct_reorder(label, baa_per_100),
    se_baa = (ci_hi - ci_lo) / (2 * 1.645)
  ) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(density_df = list(tibble(
    x       = x_grid,
    density = dnorm(x_grid, mean = baa_per_100, sd = se_baa)
  ))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(label, baa_per_100, density_df) |>
  tidyr::unnest(density_df)

block_ridge_plot <- ggplot(ridge_data,
    aes(x = x, y = label, height = density,
        fill = baa_per_100, group = label)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_ridgeline(
    scale     = 0.8,
    alpha     = 0.7,
    color     = "gray30",
    linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "firebrick",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "steelblue",
    midpoint = 0,
    guide    = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-6, 7)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Catcher blocking ability: model-implied distributions (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "Each curve is N(û_j, SE_j²) from the fitted model; width reflects uncertainty",
    x        = "Blocks above average per 100 opportunities",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 5),
    panel.grid       = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x      = element_line(color = "gray40")
  )

block_ridge_plot
```

```{r}
# paginated caterpillar CI plots — 20 catchers per page, sorted best to worst
# Full plot above is for the report; these are for legible inspection.

page_size <- 20
catcher_sorted <- catcher_effects_named |>
  arrange(desc(baa_per_100)) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number())

n_catchers <- nrow(catcher_sorted)
n_pages    <- ceiling(n_catchers / page_size)

make_caterpillar_page <- function(df, page, n_pages) {
  df |>
    mutate(
      label     = fct_reorder(label, baa_per_100),
      direction = if_else(baa_per_100 >= 0, "Above average", "Below average")
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = baa_per_100, y = label, color = direction)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
               color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
                   height = 0.4, linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Above average" = "steelblue", "Below average" = "firebrick"),
      name   = NULL
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Catcher blocking BAA/100 — page ", page, " of ", n_pages,
                        " (ranks ", (page - 1) * page_size + 1, "–",
                        min(page * page_size, n_catchers), ")"),
      subtitle = "Blocks above average per 100 opportunities; error bars = 90% CI (Laplace approx.)",
      x        = "Blocks above average per 100 opportunities",
      y        = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      legend.position    = "bottom"
    )
}

for (i in seq_len(n_pages)) {
  idx <- ((i - 1) * page_size + 1):min(i * page_size, n_catchers)
  print(make_caterpillar_page(catcher_sorted[idx, ], i, n_pages))
}
```

```{r}
# paginated ridge plots — 20 catchers per page, sorted best to worst

make_ridge_page <- function(df, page, n_pages, x_grid) {
  ridge_data <- df |>
    mutate(
      label  = fct_reorder(label, baa_per_100),
      se_baa = (ci_hi - ci_lo) / (2 * 1.645)
    ) |>
    rowwise() |>
    mutate(density_df = list(tibble(
      x       = x_grid,
      density = dnorm(x_grid, mean = baa_per_100, sd = se_baa)
    ))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    dplyr::select(label, baa_per_100, density_df) |>
    tidyr::unnest(density_df)

  ggplot(ridge_data,
      aes(x = x, y = label, height = density,
          fill = baa_per_100, group = label)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
               color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_ridgeline(scale = 1.5, alpha = 0.7,
                   color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.2) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "firebrick", mid = "white", high = "steelblue",
      midpoint = 0, guide = "none"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-6, 7)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Catcher blocking distributions — page ", page, " of ", n_pages,
                        " (ranks ", (page - 1) * page_size + 1, "–",
                        min(page * page_size, n_catchers), ")"),
      subtitle = "Each curve is N(û_j, SE_j²) from the fitted model; width reflects uncertainty",
      x        = "Blocks above average per 100 opportunities",
      y        = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid   = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_blank(),
      axis.line.x  = element_line(color = "gray40")
    )
}

for (i in seq_len(n_pages)) {
  idx <- ((i - 1) * page_size + 1):min(i * page_size, n_catchers)
  print(make_ridge_page(catcher_sorted[idx, ], i, n_pages, x_grid))
}
```

```{r}
# secondary WP/PB check
# Official wild pitches and passed balls from the des field.
# Wild pitches are charged to the pitcher; passed balls to the catcher.
# Used as a limitation-aware supplement, not the primary outcome.

wp_pb <- sc_2025_raw |>
  filter(grepl("wild pitch|passed ball", des, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>
  mutate(
    outcome    = case_when(
      grepl("wild pitch",  des, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "wild_pitch",
      grepl("passed ball", des, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "passed_ball"
    ),
    catcher_id = as.factor(fielder_2)
  )

cat("Official wild pitches:", sum(wp_pb$outcome == "wild_pitch"),  "\n")
cat("Official passed balls:", sum(wp_pb$outcome == "passed_ball"), "\n")

# Catchers with the most passed balls (catcher-charged failures)
name_lookup <- catcher_effects_named |>
  dplyr::select(catcher_id, full_name) |>
  mutate(catcher_id = as.factor(catcher_id))

wp_pb |>
  filter(outcome == "passed_ball") |>
  count(catcher_id, sort = TRUE) |>
  left_join(name_lookup, by = "catcher_id") |>
  mutate(name = if_else(is.na(full_name), as.character(catcher_id), full_name)) |>
  dplyr::select(name, n) |>
  print(n = 20)
```

# ── BASEBALL SAVANT RANKING COMPARISON ───────────────────────────────────────

```{r}
# Read Baseball Savant 2025 catcher blocking leaderboard CSV.
# Download from: https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/catcher_blocking
# Save as "savant_blocking_2025.csv" in the project folder.
# Print column names first so we can identify the right fields.

savant_raw <- read_csv("savant_blocking_2025.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
cat("Columns in Savant CSV:\n")
print(names(savant_raw))
cat("\nFirst few rows:\n")
print(head(savant_raw))
```

```{r}
# summary table: all eligible catchers, opportunities, and BAA/game estimate

catcher_effects_named |>
  arrange(desc(baa_per_100)) |>
  dplyr::select(label, n_opps, baa_per_100) |>
  mutate(baa_per_100 = round(baa_per_100, 3)) |>
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Catcher", "Blocking Opportunities", "Blocks Above Avg / 100 Opp"),
    caption   = "Catcher blocking rankings: 2025 season (minimum 100 below-zone pitches, 0.3–1.5 ft)"
  )
```

```{r}
# Join Savant rankings with our catcher effects by MLBAM player ID.
# Savant column: blocks_above_average_per_game (per game caught, same 40-opp assumption).

savant_blocking <- savant_raw |>
  dplyr::select(player_id, savant_baa_per_100 = blocks_above_average_per_game)

comparison_df <- catcher_effects_named |>
  mutate(player_id = as.integer(catcher_id)) |>
  inner_join(savant_blocking, by = "player_id") |>
  mutate(
    our_rank    = rank(-baa_per_100,         ties.method = "average"),
    savant_rank = rank(-savant_baa_per_100,  ties.method = "average")
  )

cat("Catchers matched:", nrow(comparison_df), "\n")

rho <- cor(comparison_df$our_rank, comparison_df$savant_rank, method = "spearman")
cat("Spearman rank correlation (our ranking vs. Savant):", round(rho, 3), "\n")
```

```{r}
# scatter plot: our BAA/game vs. Savant BAA/game
# Positive correlation = our simpler model recovers similar ordering.

library(ggrepel)

ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = savant_baa_per_100, y = baa_per_100, label = label)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 15) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
           label = paste0("Spearman ρ = ", round(rho, 3)),
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title    = "Our blocking model vs. Baseball Savant (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "Each point is a catcher present in both systems",
    x        = "Baseball Savant: blocks above average per game",
    y        = "Our model: blocks above average per 100 opportunities"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

# ── SAVE MODELS FOR REPORT.QMD ───────────────────────────────────────────────

```{r}
# Save fitted model and catcher effects so report.qmd can load them
# without refitting (full model takes ~5-10 min to fit).
# Run this chunk once after a successful top-to-bottom run.

saveRDS(block_mod_full,        "block_mod_full.rds")
saveRDS(catcher_effects_named, "catcher_effects_named.rds")

cat("Saved block_mod_full.rds and catcher_effects_named.rds\n")
```
